Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly discusses 'power, class, race, and gender' as factors influencing language learner identity, directly addressing inequalities and promoting social inclusion (Targets 10.2, 10.3).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly discusses 'power' dynamics (角力關係) in social contexts, which can be inferred to relate to fostering inclusive environments and non-discriminatory practices within educational institutions (Targets 16.7, 16.b).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'make the link between the international research community and our own,' directly fostering knowledge-sharing and cooperation, aligning with Target 17.6.
